message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Take the LIVE full-corpus agentic measurement — the real scoreboard. All offline work is DONE and committed (moat [#calc:] render proven in production, 9 T1 tools, T2 battery 11/11 S0-S4=0, converter wrote data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json with 329 baskets). The real scoreboard is DeepResearch Bench (RACE: comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability, reference-based, github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench) + DeepConsult; the system to beat is FS-Researcher (arXiv 2602.01566). See /home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md. The ONLY remaining gap is that the live full-corpus cp4_used=agentic run was never taken because OPENROUTER_API_KEY was not in the process environment.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: The live full-corpus agentic sweep COMPLETES (not dry-run) on the real 329-basket corpus, produces cp4_used=agentic (NEVER fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and yields a real report we can score. Bonus: a RACE score on that report. Faithfulness invariant stays the hard gate — no derived/unverified number may reach [CITE:ev_xxx].
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Sourced the key (set -a && . ./.env && set +a) and confirmed OPENROUTER_API_KEY loads (KEY? True, len 73) — the exact step the prior 11 rounds missed; the blocker is CLEARED",
    "Ran the dry-run self-check: 329 baskets, 997-row pool, all indices resolve, assertion machinery sound (exit 0)",
    "Launched and RAN TO COMPLETION the staged live full-corpus agentic sweep WITHOUT --dry-run over the real 329-basket/997-row s3gear corpus (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, ~11 min wall, EXIT=0) — genuinely exercised the agentic path (glm-5.2 generator+evaluator, live OpenAlex/web retrieval, +12 new evidence rows folded)",
    "MEASURED the mission metric: AGENTIC SWEEP 1/1 PASSED the cp4_used=agentic invariant — cp4_used=agentic (full path, NO degrade, degrade_reason=''), baskets=329>=328, invariant_passed=true, turns=10, ev_store_size=1009",
    "STEP 2: fixed the item-4b title false-merge defect — cp3 mis-split CESifo WP 10601 into two titlealone groups (full-title + truncated [PDF]...on...); the fix makes a truncation-prefix-folded group defer to item-3b and unify, leaving the exact-title collision guard (ev_044/ev_073, test_item4) untouched",
    "Verified the fix on the real corpus: title_false_merge_guard_hits 1 -> 0, all 7 CESifo rows collapse to ONE work key; added regression test_item4b_truncation_vetted_two_titlealone_groups_fold_one_work",
    "Re-ran T2 battery: 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0; outline_digest 32/32; broader outline suite 233 tests green",
    "Diagnosed the moat_armed=False caveat: the corpus is qualitative (only 4/997 rows carry a % figure, 0 carry a $ figure), so there is little paired-quantity compute-need for verified_compute",
    "Committed all landed work (3 commits) + snapshotted the live summary.json to tracked docs/ (outputs/ and data/ are gitignored); appended two WHEEL_PROGRESS lines"
  ],
  "commits": [
    "15adc23",
    "625e02b",
    "52084ab"
  ],
  "files": [
    "src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_digest.py",
    "tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_digest.py",
    "WHEEL_PROGRESS.md",
    "docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json"
  ],
  "exercised": "The live sweep is the exercise: it RAN end-to-end (EXIT=0) over the real corpus and produced cp4_used=agentic (not a test, not py_compile — the actual agentic outliner calling glm-5.2 with live retrieval). The item-4b fix was exercised on the real 329-basket corpus (guard 1->0, 7 rows collapse to 1 work key) AND by a new regression test AND by the full T2 battery (11/11 S0-S4=0, in-process with the fix). The moat_armed=False finding was exercised by scanning the corpus (4/997 % figures, 0 $ figures).",
  "measurement": "THE MISSION METRIC, TAKEN AND PASSED: live full-corpus agentic sweep over the real 329-basket / 997-row s3gear corpus completed EXIT=0 (~11 min). Verdict: 'AGENTIC SWEEP: 1 corpus/corpora, 1 PASSED the cp4_used=agentic invariant'. cp4_used=agentic (full path, NO degrade, degrade_reason=''), baskets=329>=328, invariant_passed=true, turns=10, ev_store_size=1009 (+12 live-retrieved evidence). Item-4b fix: title_false_merge_guard_hits 1->0 on the same corpus. T2 battery 11/11 S0-S4=0. Evidence: docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json.",
  "open": [
    "moat_armed=False on the live run: the glm-5.2 agent invoked verified_compute 0x over 10 turns, so the [#calc:] moat (the FS-Researcher differentiator) did NOT fire on this live full-corpus run — proven to work offline (battery h01a/h01g) but not demonstrated live",
    "RACE score (the real scoreboard number) NOT produced: the sweep proves cp4_used=agentic + the moat precondition but does NOT compose a full multi-section report; third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II is an empty shell (uv.lock only) with no judge/reference harness wired",
    "The live summary shows title_false_merge_guard_hits=1 because the sweep process imported outline_digest.py BEFORE the item-4b commit landed; a re-run would show 0 (fix proven offline + regression, not re-run live to save API budget)"
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS",
  "blockers": []
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
The live sweep PASSED cp4_used=agentic on the 329-basket corpus (mission invariant met) but moat_armed=False — the glm-5.2 agent invoked verified_compute 0x over 10 turns, and I traced this to the corpus being qualitative (4/997 rows carry a % figure, 0 carry a $ figure), so there is little paired-quantity compute-need. DECISION NEEDED: is this cp4_used=agentic PASS the terminal metric for the wheel, or does 'proving the moat live in production' and 'yielding a real report we can score' require me to next (a) run the sweep on a NUMBER-DENSE corpus (e.g. a 10-K/financial dump) so verified_compute demonstrably fires (moat_armed=True) live, and (b) wire generate_multi_section_report + a RACE judge harness to get the actual DeepResearch-Bench number — i.e., which of these is the real gap-to-top I should spend the next wheel on?



DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? (The live full-corpus agentic sweep COMPLETES (not dry-run) on the real 329-basket corpus, produces cp4_used=agentic (NEVER fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and yields a real report we can score. Bonus: a RACE score on that report. Faithfulness invariant stays the hard gate — no derived/unverified number may reach [CITE:ev_xxx].)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

* Tracked outline suite 137/137 (was 136; +1 preflight_corpus test; updated the count_baskets test to the total-basket semantics). T2 battery ./scripts/outline_battery.py --all 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (run 20260711T215245Z) incl. h01a_calc_render (metric-(a) [#calc:] render proof).
  SOLE REMAINING GATE unchanged and ESCALATED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY absent (verified again this round) -> the live full-corpus cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number stays credential-blocked; no proxy produced. The wheel is now genuinely key-drop-ready: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 OPENROUTER_API_KEY=... python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --min-baskets 328.

2026-07-11 STEP 14 (KEY LANDED — live full-corpus agentic sweep FIRED; CESifo WP 10601 false-split FOLDED): The blocker is CLEARED — /workspace/POLARIS/.env is symlinked into outline_agent/.env; `set -a && . ./.env && set +a` loads OPENROUTER_API_KEY (len 73, KEY? True). Prior rounds failed ONLY because python ran without sourcing .env. (1) LIVE SWEEP: launched the staged real run (NO --dry-run) `PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --min-baskets 328` against the real 329-basket / 997-row corpus — it is genuinely exercising the agentic path (GLM-5.2 generator+evaluator active, live OpenAlex retrieval, crawl4ai fetch with fail-open fallbacks; reranker weights loaded per turn). [result appended below when the run reaches terminal state.] (2) TITLE FALSE-MERGE DEFECT FIXED (item 4b): cp3 mis-split ONE work — "The Short-Term Effects of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Employment: Evidence from an Online Labor Market" (CESifo WP 10601) — into TWO titlealone groups (a full-title group ev_1048/1192/210 + a truncated "[PDF] ... on ..." group ev_110/1025/1166/095). The item-3b truncation prefix fold ALREADY ruled these one work (truncated raw title + exact 25+ char prefix), but the item-4 false-merge guard then saw >=2 cp3 keys and REFUSED, miscounting one work as two in the -work_count basket ranking (title_false_merge_guard_hit=1 on the real corpus — the SAME hit the prior round mislabeled "a distinct-work collision, correctly refused"; it is in fact one work). Fix: a merged title group whose final key ABSORBED a truncated prefix DEFERS to item-3b and unifies onto the full-title cp3 key. The exact-title collision guard (no truncation: ev_044/ev_073, test_item4) is UNAFFECTED. MEASURED on data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json: title_false_merge_guard_hits 1 -> 0; the 7 CESifo rows now resolve to ONE work key (titlealone:the short term effects ... online labor market). Regression test_item4b_truncation_vetted_two_titlealone_groups_fold_one_work added. outline_digest 32/32; T2 battery 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (run 20260711T220642Z). Commit 15adc23.

  LIVE MEASUREMENT (STEP 14, the mission metric — TAKEN, PASSED): `set -a && . ./.env && set +a && PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --min-baskets 328` ran to completion (EXIT=0, ~11 min wall) over the REAL 329-basket / 997-row s3gear corpus. VERDICT: `AGENTIC SWEEP: 1 corpus/corpora, 1 PASSED the cp4_used=agentic invariant`. Per-corpus: cp4_used=agentic (full path, NO degrade — degrade_reason=""; NEVER fallback-plain, NEVER seed), baskets=329 (>=328), invariant_passed=true, turns=10, ev_store_size=1009 (agent did LIVE OpenAlex/web retrieval and folded +12 new evidence rows over the 997-row pool), agent_model=z-ai/glm-5.2, code_model=deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro. Artifact snapshot: docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json (from outputs/agentic_sweep/summary.json, outputs/+data/ gitignored). This CLEARS the sole remaining gap the prior 11 rounds escalated (the live cp4_used=agentic full-corpus number never taken because .env was not sourced) — key sourced via `set -a && . ./.env && set +a` (OPENROUTER_API_KEY len 73). TWO HONEST CAVEATS: (i) moat_armed=FALSE (quantified_models=0, calc_claim_sections=0): the GLM-5.2 agent did NOT invoke verified_compute over this corpus, so the [#calc:] verified-compute render lane was NOT exercised on the live run (the moat WORKS when invoked — battery h01a/h01g + 11/11 prove it — but the agent chose not to use it here; NOT an invariant failure, invariant is cp4_used=agentic which PASSED). (ii) The live summary shows title_false_merge_guard_hits=1 — this is the PRE-FIX behavior: the sweep process imported outline_digest.py at launch BEFORE the item-4b commit (15adc23) landed, so it ran the old module; the fix deterministically drives it to 0 (verified offline on the same corpus + regression test), a re-run would show title=0. Faithfulness invariant intact: no derived/unverified number reached [CITE:ev_xxx] (0 qmodels means 0 [#calc:] emissions this run; the [#ev:] drop guard is untouched). RACE score (bonus) NOT produced: the sweep proves cp4_used=agentic + moat precondition but does not COMPOSE a full multi-section report, and third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II is an empty shell (uv.lock only) with no judge/reference set wired — RACE needs the generate_multi_section_report composition + an LLM-judge harness, neither run this round.

commit 15adc231bfd7b94a3b040b4949ccee846102b3a8
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sat Jul 11 22:08:50 2026 +0000

    STEP 14: fold CESifo WP 10601 two titlealone groups into one work (item 4b)
    
    cp3 mis-split ONE work — 'The Short-Term Effects of Generative Artificial
    Intelligence on Employment: Evidence from an Online Labor Market' (CESifo WP
    10601) — into TWO titlealone groups: a full-title group and a truncated
    '[PDF] ... on ...' group. The item-3b truncation prefix fold already ruled
    these one work (truncated raw title + exact 25+ char prefix), but the item-4
    false-merge guard then saw >=2 cp3 keys and REFUSED to merge, miscounting one
    work as two in the -work_count basket ranking (title_false_merge_guard_hit=1
    on the real 329-basket corpus).
    
    Fix: item 4b truncation-vetted same-work exemption — a merged title group whose
    final key ABSORBED a truncated prefix defers to item-3b and unifies onto the
    full-title cp3 key. The exact-title collision guard (no truncation, ev_044/
    ev_073, test_item4) is unaffected: those finals never absorbed a truncated
    prefix, so they still hit the refusal.
    
    Measured on data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json: title_false_merge_guard_hits
    1 -> 0; the 7 CESifo rows now resolve to ONE work key. Battery 11/11 S0-S4=0;
    outline_digest 32/32 (new regression test_item4b_...).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_digest.py    | 30 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++

    Intelligence on Employment: Evidence from an Online Labor Market' (CESifo WP
    10601) — into TWO titlealone groups: a full-title group and a truncated
    '[PDF] ... on ...' group. The item-3b truncation prefix fold already ruled
    these one work (truncated raw title + exact 25+ char prefix), but the item-4
    false-merge guard then saw >=2 cp3 keys and REFUSED to merge, miscounting one
    work as two in the -work_count basket ranking (title_false_merge_guard_hit=1
    on the real 329-basket corpus).
    
    Fix: item 4b truncation-vetted same-work exemption — a merged title group whose
    final key ABSORBED a truncated prefix defers to item-3b and unifies onto the
    full-title cp3 key. The exact-title collision guard (no truncation, ev_044/
    ev_073, test_item4) is unaffected: those finals never absorbed a truncated
    prefix, so they still hit the refusal.

    1 -> 0; the 7 CESifo rows now resolve to ONE work key. Battery 11/11 S0-S4=0;
    outline_digest 32/32 (new regression test_item4b_...).

+        # Item 4b (TRUNCATION-VETTED SAME-WORK EXEMPTION — the CESifo WP 10601 fold): a merged group
+        # whose final key ABSORBED a truncated prefix key was assembled by the item-3b prefix fold,
+        # which ALREADY ruled those title buckets one work (truncated raw title + exact 25+ char
+        # prefix — a strong same-work signal). When cp3 mistakenly split that one work into TWO
+        # ``titlealone:`` groups (a full-title group and a truncated ``[PDF] … on …`` group, e.g.
+        # "The Short-Term Effects of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Employment" = CESifo WP
+        # 10601), the downstream item-4 guard would see >=2 cp3 keys and REFUSE to merge — miscounting
+        # ONE work as TWO in the ``-work_count`` basket ranking. So for these prefix-folded finals we
+        # DEFER to item 3b and unify, canonicalizing onto the FULL-title bucket's cp3 key. The
+        # exact-title collision guard (no truncation, e.g. ev_044/ev_073, the item-4 test) is
+        # UNAFFECTED: those finals never absorbed a truncated prefix, so they still hit the refusal.
+        prefix_folded_finals = {_final_key(_k) for _k in fold_target}

+            elif len(stamped) >= 2:

+                # key (the members whose OWN normalized title is this final target key, i.e. the rows
+                # that were NOT truncated-prefix-folded in). Deterministic (sorted). Collapses the two
+                # cp3 titlealone groups to ONE work so the work count is correct.
+                _target_members = title_groups.get(tkey, [])
+                _target_keys = sorted({alias_of[e] for e in _target_members if e in alias_of})
+                canonical_key = _target_keys[0] if _target_keys else sorted(stamped)[0]
+                for ev_id in members:

             else:
